title: GitHub - mario/drey: One language server per workspace, shared by every editor and agent. Works with rust-analyzer, gopls, tsserver, clangd, pyright, jdtls and more: two sessions on one workspace stop paying for two indexes.
excerpt: One language server per workspace, shared by every editor and agent. Works with rust-analyzer, gopls, tsserver, clangd, pyright, jdtls and more: two sessions on one workspace stop paying for two in...
content_html:
One language server per workspace, shared by every client.

A drey is a squirrel's nest. This one is shared: many editors and coding agents crawl into a single indexed workspace instead of each digging their own.

The problem

Every LSP client starts its own language server. Two Claude Code sessions on one Rust workspace means two rust-analyzer processes, each holding a complete salsa database:

$ footprint -p 36924   # session A
phys_footprint: 3208 MB
$ footprint -p 28845   # session B
phys_footprint: 4225 MB

That is 7.4 GB to answer questions about one copy of one codebase. Add a third session, or an editor alongside the agents, and it scales linearly. The same applies to gopls, tsserver, clangd, pyright and jdtls; Rust is the example because it is the one with the measurement.

drey makes them share.

Install

brew install mario/drey/drey     # macOS, brings its own Rust toolchain
cargo install drey               # anywhere with a Rust toolchain

Then put drey in front of every language server on this machine, for every client at once:

drey install                     # --dry-run first, if you want to see the plan

That writes a wrapper named after each language server into ~/.drey/bin and prepends it to PATH, so your existing clients pick drey up without being reconfigured. Open a new shell and check:

which rust-analyzer              # ~/.drey/bin/rust-analyzer
drey status                      # what is running, and who is attached

To point one client at drey by hand instead, name drey serve rust-analyzer wherever that client currently names rust-analyzer.

docs/install.md covers configuring servers, watching what is running, uninstalling, and what to do when something looks wrong.

How it works

drey is one binary in two roles. The shim looks exactly like a language server on stdio. The daemon owns a pool of real servers, one per workspace, and autostarts on first use.

  Claude Code ──▶ drey serve ─┐
  Codex       ──▶ drey serve ─┼──▶ daemon ──▶ one rust-analyzer
  Helix       ──▶ drey serve ─┘

A client attaches to a running server when the server name and initializationOptions match and its workspace roots are contained in the roots already indexed. Containment rather than equality means opening a single crate inside a Cargo workspace costs nothing: it attaches to the workspace already in memory. Git worktrees and branches deliberately stay separate, because merging them would produce wrong answers, which is worse than using memory.

Two clients with different unsaved edits to the same file still share one process. Each document keeps the text everyone agrees on plus whichever client's edits the server is currently holding, and the daemon swaps that before answering. Verified against a real rust-analyzer: with one client holding pub size: u32 and another holding u64, the same process reports size = 4, align = 0x4 to the first and size = 8, align = 0x8 to the second. That is a type layout recomputed per client, not an echo of the text they sent.

docs/architecture.md explains the three ideas the design rests on, and the parts that were harder than they look.

Status

Early. It works and it is tested, but it has been exercised against a mock server far more than against every real one.

The state swapping rests on an assumption worth stating plainly. It needs the server to process messages in order, which every LSP server must, and to either snapshot per request (rust-analyzer does) or answer before the next swap arrives. A server that batches work across messages could in principle answer against a state that has since moved. If you hit that, lowering the thrash threshold makes such a client fork instead.

A swap also resends full document text rather than an incremental edit. That is cheap for source files and would not be for very large generated ones.

Prior art

lspmux does the same job and got there first; lspmux-rust-analyzer packages it for this exact use case. drey exists because of the two things they document as out of scope: server-initiated requests are dropped there, and document divergence between clients is not reconciled.

Contributing

Bug reports, patches and new server builtins are welcome. Read CONTRIBUTING.md first: it covers the checks CI runs on every pull request, and what a change to sharing policy needs before it lands.

Licence

Apache License 2.0. Copyright 2026 Mario Đanić. See LICENSE and NOTICE.

content_html_zh:
每个工作区一台语言服务器，由每个客户端共享。

Drey 是松鼠的巢。 这是共享的：许多编辑器和编码代理爬入单个索引工作区，而不是各自挖掘自己的工作区。

问题

每个 LSP 客户端都会启动自己的语言服务器。 一个 Rust 工作区上的两个 Claude Code 会话意味着两个 rust-analyzer 进程，每个进程都拥有一个完整的 salsa 数据库：

$ footprint -p 36924   # session A
phys_footprint: 3208 MB
$ footprint -p 28845   # session B
phys_footprint: 4225 MB

回答有关一个代码库的一份副本的问题需要 7.4 GB。 添加第三个会话，或与代理一起添加编辑器，它会线性扩展。 这同样适用于 gopls、tsserver、clangd、pyright 和 jdtls； Rust 就是一个例子，因为它是具有测量值的例子。

德雷让他们分享。

安装

brew install mario/drey/drey     # macOS, brings its own Rust toolchain
cargo install drey               # anywhere with a Rust toolchain

然后将 drey 放在这台机器上每个语言服务器的前面，对于每个客户端来说：

drey install                     # --dry-run first, if you want to see the plan

这将以每种语言服务器命名的包装器写入 ~/.drey/bin 并将其添加到 PATH 之前，因此您现有的客户端无需重新配置即可选择 drey。 打开一个新的 shell 并检查：

which rust-analyzer              # ~/.drey/bin/rust-analyzer
drey status                      # what is running, and who is attached

要手动将某个客户端指向 drey，请在该客户端当前命名为 rust-analyzer 的任何位置命名为 dreyserve rust-analyzer。

docs/install.md 涵盖配置服务器、查看正在运行的内容、卸载以及出现问题时应采取的措施。

它是如何运作的

德雷是一位具有两种角色的二元人物。 该垫片看起来与 stdio 上的语言服务器一模一样。 该守护进程拥有一个真实服务器池，每个工作区一个，并在第一次使用时自动启动。

  Claude Code ──▶ drey serve ─┐
  Codex       ──▶ drey serve ─┼──▶ daemon ──▶ one rust-analyzer
  Helix       ──▶ drey serve ─┘

当服务器名称和初始化选项匹配并且其工作区根包含在已索引的根中时，客户端将连接到正在运行的服务器。 遏制而不是平等意味着在 Cargo 工作区中打开单个 crate 不需要任何成本：它附加到内存中已有的工作区。 Git 工作树和分支故意保持分离，因为合并它们会产生错误的答案，这比使用内存更糟糕。

对同一文件进行不同的未保存编辑的两个客户端仍然共享一个进程。 每个文档都保留每个人都同意的文本以及服务器当前保存的客户端编辑，并且守护进程在应答之前交换该文本。 针对真实的 rust 分析器进行验证：一个客户端持有 pub size：u32，另一个客户端持有 u64，同一进程向第一个客户端报告 size = 4，align = 0x4，向第二个客户端报告 size = 8，align = 0x8。 这是每个客户端重新计算的类型布局，而不是他们发送的文本的回显。

docs/architecture.md 解释了设计所依据的三个想法，以及比看起来更难的部分。

地位

早期的。 它可以工作并且经过测试，但它在模拟服务器上的测试次数远远多于在真实服务器上的测试次数。

状态交换基于一个值得明确说明的假设。 它需要服务器按顺序处理消息（每个 LSP 服务器都必须这样做），并为每个请求创建快照（rust-analyzer 会这样做）或在下一个交换到达之前进行应答。 原则上，跨消息批处理工作的服务器可以针对已移动的状态进行响应。 如果你遇到了这个问题，降低阈值就会产生这样的客户端分叉。

交换还会重新发送完整的文档文本，而不是增量编辑。 这对于源文件来说很便宜，但对于生成的非常大的文件来说就不那么容易了。

现有技术

lspmux 做同样的工作并且首先到达那里； lspmux-rust-analyzer 将其打包用于这个确切的用例。 drey 的存在是因为他们记录的两件事超出了范围：服务器发起的请求被丢弃在那里，并且客户端之间的文档分歧没有得到协调。

贡献

欢迎错误报告、补丁和新的服务器内置程序。 首先阅读 CONTRIBUTING.md：它涵盖了 CI 在每个拉取请求上运行的检查，以及共享策略在落地之前需要进行哪些更改。

执照
